summary:
The agent considers familial history and ethnicity, noting that while superficial spreading melanoma is global majority, acral lentiginous melanoma is more commonly observed in African Americans. Due to the patient’s race and potential high UV exposure through travels to tropical regions, even without visible symptoms, acral lentiguosus melanoma becomes the most clinically appropriate choice considering demographic risks and geographic factors.  

Despite initial hesitation due to insufficient specific information, the agent concludes that acral lentiginous melanoma aligns better than other subtypes with both racial predisposition and environmental risk profiles present in this case.
